calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Successor Trustee'S First Accounting
Off Calendar Nov-25-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Rowena Navia, appearing via Zoom as counsel for Petitioner Adnan Khan.

The court addressed the status of the first accounting. Counsel report that the trustee requires additional time to prepare the accounting due to complexities involving two sub-trusts (Survivor's Trust and Exemption Trust). Transactions between the trusts need to be resolved to ensure accuracy. Counsel requested an additional 30 to 45 days to prepare.

The court orders the status hearing off calendar. Status hearings for the Survivor's Trust (PTR-24-306980) and Exemption Trust (PTR-24-306982) are scheduled for a hearing on January 6, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Deputy: Singh; Not reported (D204)

MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-18-302064 Conservatorship of Hajra N. Kahn;
PTR-18-302065 The Abdul Aziz Kahn and Hajra Nighat Khan Joint Living Trust;
PTR-24-306980 The Survivor's Trust Created Under The Abdul Aziz Kahn and Hajra Nighat Khan Joint Living Trust;
PTR-24-306982 The Exemption Trust Created Under The Abdul Aziz Kahn and Hajra Nighat Khan Joint Living Trust

Appearances: Marta Eisenberg appeared by zoom for Debra Dolch (former conservator of the estate and former trustee).

Mrs. Eisenberg confirmed that Mrs. Dolch has transferred all of the assets to Adnan Khan, stated that she has not heard from any of the children regarding any issues of the accounting and that her client and Mr. Khan are in communication. The Third and Final Account and Report of Former Conservator of the Estate, etc., the Third and Final Account and Report of Former Successor Trustee, etc. (joint living trust), the First and Final Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc. (survivor's trust), and the First and Final Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc. (exemption trust) are under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Tan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Jun-30-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-18-302064 Conservatorship of Hajra N. Khan and
PTR-18-302065 The Abdul Aziz Khan and Hajra Nighat Khan Joint Living Trust.

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg appeared by zoom for Debra Dolch (conservator/trustee).

The court stated that it is okay with not allocating the assets to the separate trust for this accounting, but for the next accounting/third accounting they will need to be allocated and the parties will need to open another case/trust case number for the exemption trust. The court also noted neither the Mangels property nor the Olmstead property have any income and that it should be kind of clean to cut. Ms. Friedeberg stated that it makes sense, that they noticed there was about a one-month period before the order was signed by the court allocating the assets, and that they will probably do a mini one-month accounting, and then do a full accounting of the two sub-trusts. The Second Account and Report of Conservator, etc., and the Second Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc. are under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: H. Singh. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jan-14-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Marta Eisenberg, via ZOOM, for Debra Dolch, Trustee.

The court advises Ms. Eisenberg of its tentative ruling to grant her petition excluding paragraphs one and two. Ms. Eisenberg reports on what her intention was in including those paragraphs but has no objection to the court's tentative ruling.

The Petition for Order Confirming Assets of the Sub Trusts is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: S. Saephan. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Funding Of The Sub-Trusts
Off Calendar Mar-16-2021 Continued To May-18-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Marta Eisenberg appeared by zoom for and with Debra Dolch (Trustee).

The Court asked if there is an exemption trust. Ms. Eisenberg stated that they discovered that in early 2011 Ms. Kahn engaged legal Counsel for advice regarding funding of the sub-trusts, that Ms. Kahn executed deeds funding the real property into the sub-trusts, that they attempted to communicate with the drafting Attorney, but he passed away and that they do not have sufficient records or information of any other trust assets. Ms. Eisenberg also stated that the funding was undertaken by Ms. Kahn as the Trustee at the time of her Husband's death and that they are continuing to look for any other information. The Court noted that they will need two different trust case numbers. Ms. Eisenberg stated that the bypass trust has two parcels of property that are not income producing and that the children live in the property. With respect to the survivor's trust, Ms. Eisenberg stated that all of the real property is included in their accounting, that the accounting was approved, that they could bifurcate the two trusts and that the next accounting is due in 2022. Status on funding of the sub-trusts is continued to May 18, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the parties to get more information and try and get on the same page as to what to expect going forward. Ms. Eisenberg is to provide an update in writing.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Jan-19-2021 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees
Off Calendar Jan-19-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-18-302064 Conservatorship of Hajra N. Kahn and
PTR-18-302065 The Abdul Aziz Kahn and Hajra Nighat Khan Joint Living Trust.

Appearances: Marta Eisenberg appeared by zoom for Debra Dolch (Conservator/Successor Trustee).
Rowena Navia appeared by zoom for and with Adnan Kahn (Former Trustee) by zoom.

The Court asked what's the point of the pay-on death accounts. Ms. Eisenberg stated that Ms. Kahn established the accounts prior to the conservatorship being established, that at that time Ms. Kahn designated the accounts as pay-on-death accounts, that the beneficiaries are family members and that these will transfer by operation of law to the beneficiaries she has designated. Ms. Eisenberg also stated that one account has a balance of $8,977.00, that the other account has a balance of $22,035.00 and that the combined value is $31,000.00. In addition, Ms. Eisenberg stated that these accounts were recipients of the social security benefit and rental income, that at the time of inception of the conservatorship the amounts were at $31,000.00 and that these amounts will remain. Furthermore, Ms. Eisenberg addressed the issue of funding of the sub-trusts stating that it is time for an analysis as to whether the exemption trusts should be funded, or seek instruction from the Court, and that since the time of Ms. Kahn's Husband's death they have been dealing with preserving assets from a Nephew, Ms. Kahn's care needs and conflict with the Agent that was making medical decisions. Ms. Navia stated that she has nothing to add. The First Account and Report of Conservator, etc., the Amended First and Final Account and Report of Temporary Successor Trustee, etc., and the First Account Report of Successor Trustee, etc. are under submission. Status on funding of the sub-trusts is set for March 16, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 in the trust matter.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-18-302064 Conservatorship of Hajra N. Kahn and
PTR-18-302365 The Abdul Aziz Kahn and Hajra Nighat Khan Joint Living Trust.

Appearances: Melanie Emmons for Debra Dolch.

Ms. Emmons requested a two to four-week continuance stating that they have all of the materials and that she wants to make sure they have enough time to do the accountings. Status on filing of the first accountings are continued to April 7, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.
